Prompt

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the support inbox with a query related to "support" or by listing emails in the support folder using search_emails or list_emails to discover unanswered client emails. Agent should find: (a) Marcus Webb's first email (email_email_ca010e9c9446, subject: "Checking in on my car delivery status") sent to support@moveops.com asking about his 2019 Honda Civic shipment to Boston with no support response, (b) Marcus Webb's frustrated second follow-up (email_email_87f575fcacf9, subject: "Second follow-up: I need an actual ETA for my car") also to support@moveops.com, CC'd to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com, with no support response, and (c) Jae-won Kim's email (email_email_1a3fb965161a, subject: "Lease paperwork for Austin apartment") sent to support@moveops.com asking for final lease documents. Omar previously replied to this one (email_email_5abaeedf3e7e) promising to follow up with the relocation team but never did.
OE 2: Browse the #customer-support Slack channel (channel_id: C007) using conversations_history, or search with conversations_search_messages for messages in that channel, to discover items that were logged but never picked up. Key findings: (a) Julian Brooks posted on April 13 that Emilia Cruz called about the scratched Steinway piano from her NorthWind move, asked for a same-day callback, and Julian wrote "Leaving this here for pickup" the message has zero replies, nobody followed up. (b) Julian posted on April 24 that he was "taking the two BrightLoop misses" (Marcus Webb car and Simone Richter apartment), indicating support had already failed on those items. (c) Mina Hashimoto's StormCloud reconstruction messages from April 24-25 mention Omar's name in connection with the Jae-won Kim triage.
OE 3: Look up client companies using crm_search_companies to determine account ownership for each discovered item. Search for "BrightLoop" to find BrightLoop Analytics (company_brightloop, AM: Mina Hashimoto), search for "StormCloud" to find StormCloud Analytics (company_stormcloud, AM: Mina Hashimoto), and search for "NorthWind" to find NorthWind Technologies (company_northwind, AM: Catalina Dubois). These CRM lookups determine who each item should be routed to.
OE 4: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records in the Relocations table (tblRelocations01, base appMoveOpsOps001) with field_name "Name" and value "Jae-won Kim" to verify his relocation status. The record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin) shows Status "In Progress", Origin "Seattle, WA", Destination "Austin, TX", Assigned Coordinator "Suki Patel", with Special Requirements noting "Lease still unsigned UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed." This reveals the lease was never finalized and the false completion status misled Omar's earlier triage.
OE 5: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records in the Relocations table with field_name "Name" and value "Marcus Webb" to find his current BrightLoop relocation (recMarcusWebbBrightloop). The record shows Status "In Progress", Origin "Atlanta", Destination "Boston", Assigned Coordinator "Suki Patel", with Special Requirements noting vehicle shipping for a 2019 Honda Civic via Road Runner Auto Transport as an add-on to the relocation.
OE 6: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Jae-won" or "StormCloud" or "support" (or similar) to find existing support tickets. Agent should find linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 (title: "Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents", priority 4, labels: Support, Low Priority, StormCloud). This is the ticket Omar created from the original triage that needs routing notes added.
OE 7: Look up account manager contact information using contacts_search_contacts with queries for "Mina Hashimoto" and "Catalina Dubois" to retrieve their email addresses (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, catalina.dubois@moveops.com) before forwarding emails. Also verify that client contacts Marcus Webb (marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com), Jae-won Kim (jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io), and Emilia Cruz (emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com) exist in the contacts system all three are already present.
OE 8: Reply to Jae-won Kim's email (email_email_1a3fb965161a) using reply_to_email, sent from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. Content should acknowledge that Omar's earlier response did not result in the promised follow-through, that the lease situation has been escalated to the account manager, and that someone from the account team will be in touch with a real update on the Austin apartment status.
OE 9: Forward Marcus Webb's support email (email_email_ca010e9c9446 or email_email_87f575fcacf9) to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com using forward_email, sent from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. The forwarding context should note that Marcus is a BrightLoop employee with two unanswered emails about his 2019 Honda Civic delivery being stuck in transit, that Julian has already sent a direct reply stepping in on this, and that this needs account-level follow-through.
OE 10: Send an email from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to catalina.dubois@moveops.com using send_email about the Emilia Cruz callback that Julian logged in #customer-support on April 13 but nobody picked up. Content should explain that Emilia Cruz from NorthWind Technologies called about damage to her Steinway piano during her relocation, requested a same-day callback, and that the callback was never made. This needs to go to Catalina as NorthWind's account manager.
OE 11: Update the Linear ticket linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 using linear_update_issue to add routing context that the Jae-won Kim issue has been escalated to the StormCloud account manager (Mina Hashimoto), that the original low-priority triage was based on incorrect Airtable completion data, and the current status of the issue.
OE 12: Send an email from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com to julian.brooks@moveops.com using send_email summarizing what was found in the inbox and support channel and how each item was routed: Marcus Webb car delivery emails forwarded to Mina Hashimoto (BrightLoop AM), Jae-won Kim lease situation forwarded to Mina Hashimoto (StormCloud AM) with a corrected reply sent to Jae-won, and Emilia Cruz callback sent to Catalina Dubois (NorthWind AM).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 A reply or email was sent (via reply_to_email or send_email) to [jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io](mailto:jaewon.kim@stormcloud.io) from [omar.ibrahim@moveops.com](mailto:omar.ibrahim@moveops.com) acknowledging that the earlier response did not result in follow-through, or that the lease or apartment situation has been escalated (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #1The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_email_1a3fb965161a' (Jae-won Kim's email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. The content explicitly acknowledges that the lease was never finalized, that it's being escalated to Mina, and that temporary housing stays in place. This directly addresses the broken promise and provides a real update.
✓Run #2The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_email_1a3fb965161a' from 'omar.ibrahim@moveops.com'. The content acknowledges the situation and informs Jae-won that 'your housing situation has been escalated and your account manager Mina Hashimoto is actively working on it,' addressing the prior missed follow-through.
✓Run #3The agent replied to email_email_1a3fb965161a (Jae-won Kim's email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. While it doesn't explicitly call out the prior missed follow-up, it does reference 'your Austin placement and the UrbanNest coordination' and escalates the lease paperwork situation to Mina Hashimoto for a 'complete update.' The criterion allows '(or similar)' and the escalation of the lease/apartment situation is satisfied.
✓Run #4The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_email_1a3fb965161a' (Jae-won Kim's email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. The content explicitly acknowledges the delay ('I apologize for the delay in getting back to you'), explains the situation was due to incorrect Airtable data, and states that Mina Hashimoto would be reaching out with a full update on his Austin housing/lease situation.
✓Run #5The agent used reply_to_email with email_id 'email_email_1a3fb965161a' and sender 'omar.ibrahim@moveops.com'. The content explicitly acknowledges 'the final lease documents for your Austin apartment have not yet been fully executed on our end' and apologizes 'for the delay in getting back to you', signaling the broken promise and escalating to Mina as a priority.
✓Run #6The agent called reply_to_email with email_id 'email_email_1a3fb965161a' (Jae-won Kim's email) from omar.ibrahim@moveops.com. This satisfies the criterion.

The Julian email only vaguely references BrightLoop via Tessa Moreno, not Marcus Webb's car issue.
